title: Request Targets, Host Authority, Proxy Trust, and Client Identity · Type-System Trace: Forwarded Chain Traversal
summary: An origin trusts arbitrary Host and X-Forwarded values, builds reset links from the request, rate-limits the proxy address, and logs raw URLs containing secrets. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts forwarded chain traversal increment in request authority and trusted-proxy decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Traverse Forwarded or X-Forwarded chains from the trusted socket boundary and reject malformed, excessive, or topology-inconsistent claims.
Configure trust proxy by explicit addresses or hop logic and derive client, protocol, and host only when every trusted hop overwrites forwarded fields.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Forwarded Chain Traversal · Theory Forwarded Chain Traversal
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 7949 in a browser application using cookie authentication across approved origins. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to derive authority only across an explicit trusted-proxy and host allow-list boundary; keep peak memory below the stated byte ceiling; then abort during a dependency phase and preserve cleanup plus response ownership. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Forwarded Chain Traversal contract for the supplied data.
